title: Text to Slug Converter
description: Turn any title or phrase into a clean URL slug. Supports hyphen/underscore separators and accented characters.
placeholder: Enter text to convert, e.g. Hello World & Friends!

results.empty: Enter text above to generate a slug

howToUse.label: Enter text
howToUse.description: Type or paste the title, phrase, or heading you want to convert.

howToUse.label: Choose options
howToUse.description: Select your preferred separator (hyphen or underscore) and other formatting options.

howToUse.label: Copy the slug
howToUse.description: The slug updates in real time. Click "Copy" to copy it to your clipboard.

faq.question: What is a URL slug?
faq.answer: A URL slug is the human-readable part of a URL that identifies a specific page. For example, in https://example.com/blog/hello-world, the slug is "hello-world". Good slugs are short, descriptive, and lowercase.

faq.question: What characters are removed?
faq.answer: Special characters (punctuation, symbols), leading/trailing hyphens, and consecutive hyphens are removed or collapsed. Accented characters (é, ü, ñ, etc.) are converted to their ASCII equivalents.

faq.question: Hyphen vs underscore — which should I use?
faq.answer: Google recommends hyphens for URL slugs, treating them as word separators. Underscores join words together in Google's view. For SEO, hyphens are the safe default.

faq.question: Does this tool handle Japanese or other non-Latin text?
faq.answer: Non-Latin characters that cannot be mapped to ASCII are removed from the slug. For multilingual URLs, consider URL-encoding the original text instead.
